################################################################################
# batch_am.txt — gameplay C1->C2 drain (campaign batch 2 of ~5)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-03.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool1,2,3,7,8,10 pre-assigned)
#               -> pool0,4,5,6 SKIPPED: all four carry an ACTIVE .lock right now
#                  (pool4 chronic; pool0/5/6 leaked in-JVM locks during batch_al's
#                  fanout — see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock). The six
#                  assigned slots are the ones that completed batch_al cleanly.
# Total RVAs:   156  (gameplay, next-156 by address 0x0041e140..0x00455fe0)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 26 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ak/al (Opus author-only fanout
#               + scripted ghidra-sweep + central re-classify).
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: gameplay is now a 636-RVA C1 pool (was 792; batch_al drained the
# lowest 156). This is batch 2 of ~5. It takes the NEXT 156 by address above
# 0x0041d910 (the 0x0041e/0x0041f/0x00420/0x00421/0x00422 dense cluster + a 0x00449-
# 0x00455 tail). batch_al observed that the upper 0x0041 region is effect/render-
# heavy (s4 reclassed 13->render+6->hud; s6 reclassed all 26->render). EXPECT the
# same here: many of these "gameplay" candidates will reclassify to render / hud /
# effects on decomp. That's fine — they leave gameplay C1 regardless. Confirm per
# RVA and record subsystem_observed. render (458) remains the OTHER campaign,
# deferred (its 0x004e/0x0053/0x0054/0x0055 pages are RenderWare-core-heavy and need
# their own library-aware batches).
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-03):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_al fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-0334; commits 29188277 drain + 9cf1e64e release + 96375246
#     re-classify). gameplay C1 792 -> 636. SAFE to fan out. (If a sweep is running
#     when you start, STOP and re-confirm.)
#   * Library-band screen over all 156 candidates: ZERO hits (CRT 0x004a0000-
#     0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-0x004fc9e0 | qhull 0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820).
#     Still apply the library-confirm rule if any RVA decodes as a named
#     RenderWare/CRT primitive (note it, do NOT rename, set library_skip=<rva>:<tag>).
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj/ak/al
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — batch_aj
#     s3 used `## Purpose` and the sweep manifest builder returned 0 plated for
#     that bucket until renamed. This heading is a HARD requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=` — batch_ak s3 used `rvas_c2=` and the builder
#     could not see it). If you have library-skips, put genuine-game RVAs in
#     `rvas=` and list vendored ones in a SEPARATE `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...`
#     field for the central re-classify (they are NOT plated by the sweep).
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section with the gap described; central
#     re-classify mints from the reserved range (this batch: U-8000..U-8299,
#     S-6400..S-6599).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_am s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (a sibling parallel session may
#     have staged unrelated files; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5,
#     the batch_aj-s5 shared-working-copy index race).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (confirm per-bucket plated == queued rva counts AND missing_md==0).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; honor subsystem_observed
#        reclassifications; reclass-OUT any library_skip RVAs to
#        third-party-library[...] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs from the reserved ranges.
#        Merge fragments into SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_am_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). pool0/4/5/6 are SKIPPED (active .lock at generation
#   time; see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock). If your assigned slot is
#   stale/leaked-locked, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free clone (NOT pool0/4/5/6;
#   try pool9/11/12/13...), and record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row.
#   ALWAYS program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 156: gameplay. The 0x0041e-0x00422 + 0x00449-0x00455
#   region mixes game-state/race logic with on-screen-effect / HUD-overlay rendering
#   (RenderWare clump/atomic/material). Based on batch_al, EXPECT a meaningful share
#   to reclass to render / hud / effects. CONFIRM per RVA from decomp and record
#   subsystem_observed=<X> in the queue row when it differs. The sweep + central
#   re-classify reconcile.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_gameplay_0041e140_0041efe0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0041e140_0041efe0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First slice of the gameplay batch-2 pool (0x0041e140-0x0041efe0). Read each
end-to-end and write a mechanical C2 plate (full control flow, every offset/constant
cited, callees + callers noted). Working hypothesis: gameplay; expect some
render/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm or reclassify per RVA.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (deepen, don't restart). Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (next gameplay range).

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (above). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_am_s1";
   use Mashed_pool1 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Write re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0041e140_0041efe0/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-03) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint).
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s1.md: date, am_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   plated>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed + reclassifications +
   library_skip=... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_am s1): author 26 C1->C2 plates
   for gameplay 0041e140_0041efe0".

# Candidates (26)
0041e140,0041e150,0041e170,0041e1c0,0041e1e0,0041e4b0,0041e8d0,0041e8e0,0041e900,0041e910,0041e920,0041e930,0041e940,0041e950,0041e9f0,0041ea00,0041ea20,0041ea30,0041ea40,0041ea50,0041ea60,0041ea70,0041ec00,0041ecc0,0041eeb0,0041efe0

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA is a library family (memcpy/_ftol2/RW primitive) — note in plate, do NOT
  rename; set subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva>:<tag>
  in the queue row.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_gameplay_0041f060_004210f0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0041f060_004210f0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s2.md

# Mission
Second gameplay slice (0x0041f060-0x004210f0). Working hypothesis: gameplay;
expect render/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_am_s2", Mashed_pool2,
bucket dir bucket_gameplay_0041f060_004210f0/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_am s2): author 26 C1->C2 plates
for gameplay 0041f060_004210f0".

# Candidates (26)
0041f060,0041f0d0,0041f100,0041f220,0041f290,0041f2c0,0041f300,0041f330,0041f360,0041f3d0,0041f4f0,0041f590,0041f710,0041f770,0041fe10,00420290,00420340,00420870,00420d80,00420da0,00420dc0,00420e00,00421060,00421080,004210b0,004210f0

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.

================================================================================
Session 4 — bucket_gameplay_00422440_0044e070  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool7 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00422440_0044e070/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s4.md

# Mission
Fourth gameplay slice. NOTE the wide address span: one RVA at 0x00422440 then a
jump to the 0x00449-0x0044e cluster (no gameplay-C1 rows sit between 0x004224 and
0x004499 — they are already promoted or other subsystems). These 26 are the next
gameplay-C1 by address regardless. Working hypothesis: gameplay; expect
render/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_am_s4", Mashed_pool7, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00422440_0044e070/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s4.md,
commit "scribe(batch_am s4): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00422440_0044e070".

# Candidates (26)

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth gameplay slice (0x00454130-0x00455fe0). Working hypothesis: gameplay;
expect render/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA. Closes the
next-156 slice of the gameplay campaign (batch 2/~5).

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_am_s6", Mashed_pool10, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00454130_00455fe0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_am_s6.md,
commit "scribe(batch_am s6): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00454130_00455fe0".

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


################################################################################
# END batch_am.txt — 156 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions (gameplay campaign 2/~5).
# After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header), then
# central re-classify C1->C2 + merge per-session queue fragments into
# re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained. Expected: gameplay C1 636 -> ~480 (minus any
# reclassified to render/hud/effects/boot/save). Remaining campaigns after this
# slice: gameplay (~480) + render (458).
################################################################################
